questions.q: In which Indian city is the Red Fort located?
questions.explanation: The Red Fort stands in Delhi, on the banks of the Yamuna river.

questions.q: The Red Fort was built during which imperial era?
questions.explanation: The Red Fort is a Mughal-era fortress, built in the seventeenth century.

questions.q: From the ramparts of the Red Fort, who hoists the national flag each Independence Day?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister of India hoists the national flag from the Red Fort each Independence Day.

questions.q: The Red Fort served as the setting for which famous post-war court-martials?
questions.explanation: The INA trials of 1945-46 were held at the Red Fort, lending them the name 'Red Fort Trials.'

questions.q: The Red Fort is widely regarded as a symbol of what?
questions.explanation: The Red Fort has become a powerful symbol of Indian sovereignty and national pride.

questions.q: By which Hindi or Urdu name is the Red Fort also known?
questions.explanation: The Red Fort is known in Hindi and Urdu as the Lal Qila, meaning 'Red Fort.'

questions.q: The Red Fort takes its name from the colour of which building material?
questions.explanation: The fort is named for its massive walls of red sandstone.

questions.q: On which river's banks was the Red Fort originally built?
questions.explanation: The Red Fort was built beside the Yamuna river in Delhi.

questions.q: On India's first Independence Day, the national flag was raised over the Red Fort in which year?
questions.explanation: The tricolour was hoisted at the Red Fort as India became independent in 1947.

questions.q: During the freedom struggle, the Red Fort gained prominence partly because the British used it for what purpose?
questions.explanation: The British court-martialled captured Indian National Army officers there in 1945-46.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor commissioned the construction of the Red Fort?
questions.explanation: The Red Fort was commissioned by Emperor Shah Jahan, who also built the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: The Red Fort was built when Shah Jahan moved his capital to a new city named what?
questions.explanation: The fort was the centrepiece of Shahjahanabad, Shah Jahan's new capital in Delhi.

questions.q: Construction of the Red Fort was completed around which year?
questions.explanation: The Red Fort was completed around 1648 after roughly a decade of construction.

questions.q: During the Revolt of 1857, the Red Fort was the seat of which symbolic rebel leader?
questions.explanation: The last Mughal emperor Bahadur Shah Zafar was proclaimed leader of the 1857 rebels at the Red Fort in Delhi.

questions.q: After the British suppressed the 1857 revolt, what did they do at and around the Red Fort?
options: Damaged and demolished parts of it and stationed troops there
questions.explanation: Following 1857 the British demolished many structures within the fort and turned it into a military garrison.

questions.q: Subhas Chandra Bose's INA gave a famous slogan expressing the aim of marching to the Red Fort. What was it?
questions.explanation: The INA's battle cry 'Chalo Dilli,' meaning 'March to Delhi,' aimed at reaching the Red Fort, the seat of power.

questions.q: The first Prime Minister to hoist the flag and address the nation from the Red Fort on Independence Day was whom?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru, India's first Prime Minister, raised the flag and addressed the nation from the Red Fort in 1947.

questions.q: Which renowned audience hall inside the Red Fort was used by the Mughal emperor for public petitions?
questions.explanation: The Diwan-i-Aam, or Hall of Public Audience, is one of the celebrated structures within the Red Fort.

questions.q: The INA trials at the Red Fort drew public attention partly because the venue itself symbolized what?
options: Indian sovereignty and former imperial power
questions.explanation: Holding the trials at the Red Fort, a symbol of Indian sovereignty, heightened their nationalist resonance.

questions.q: In 2007 the Red Fort received which form of international recognition?
questions.explanation: The Red Fort complex was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2007.

questions.q: Construction of the Red Fort began around which year under Shah Jahan?
questions.explanation: Work on the Red Fort began around 1638-1639 and continued for roughly a decade.

questions.q: Which famous jewelled throne, later looted, was originally housed in the Red Fort's Diwan-i-Khas?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan's celebrated Peacock Throne stood in the Diwan-i-Khas of the Red Fort before it was carried off by Nadir Shah in 1739.

questions.q: Whose invasion in 1739 led to the looting of the Red Fort, including the Peacock Throne?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah of Persia sacked Delhi in 1739 and carried away the Peacock Throne from the Red Fort.

questions.q: A celebrated Persian couplet inscribed in the Red Fort's Diwan-i-Khas declares that if there is a paradise on earth, where is it?
options: It is here, it is here, it is here
questions.explanation: The famous inscription reads, 'If there is a paradise on earth, it is this, it is this, it is this.'

questions.q: After the 1857 trial at the Red Fort, Bahadur Shah Zafar was exiled to which city?
questions.explanation: Bahadur Shah Zafar was tried after 1857 and exiled to Rangoon, where he died in 1862.

questions.q: Which principal gateway of the Red Fort faces the historic Chandni Chowk market area?
questions.explanation: The Lahori Gate is the main gate of the Red Fort and faces toward Chandni Chowk; the Prime Minister speaks from its rampart.

questions.q: The elegant marble pavilions along the Red Fort's eastern side overlooking the river were collectively part of what palace complex?
options: The imperial private palaces (such as Rang Mahal and Khas Mahal)
questions.explanation: The riverfront held the emperor's private palaces, including the Rang Mahal and Khas Mahal, within the Red Fort.

questions.q: Who is traditionally credited as a chief architect associated with the building of the Red Fort and Shahjahanabad?
questions.explanation: Ustad Ahmad Lahori, also linked with the Taj Mahal, is traditionally credited with the Red Fort's design.

questions.q: A white marble mosque added inside the Red Fort by Aurangzeb is known by what name?
questions.explanation: The Moti Masjid, or Pearl Mosque, a small marble mosque, was built inside the Red Fort by Aurangzeb.

questions.q: The water channel that once ran through the Red Fort's palaces carrying flowing water was poetically called what?
questions.explanation: A channel called the Nahr-i-Bihisht, or 'Stream of Paradise,' once flowed through the Red Fort's private palaces.
